name: Edinburgh
summary: Edinburgh ranks among the UK's safest cities with crime rates of just 67.9 per 1,000 people, well below the national average. The city features excellent police presence, well-lit streets, and a strong safety infrastructure that makes it highly secure for tourists, including solo travelers and women. While petty theft can occur in crowded tourist areas, violent crime against visitors is extremely rare.
quickVerdict: Edinburgh is exceptionally safe for tourists with remarkably low crime rates and excellent safety infrastructure. The US State Department maintains a Level 2 advisory for the UK due to general terrorism concerns, but Edinburgh specifically enjoys one of the lowest crime rates in Britain. The city's well-organized public safety system, frequent police patrols around major attractions, and welcoming local culture create an ideal environment for all types of travelers. Most areas remain safe day and night, though standard urban precautions are always advisable.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town/Royal Mile
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart featuring Edinburgh Castle and major attractions. Well-patrolled with good lighting and high foot traffic, though pickpocketing can occur during peak tourist season.

neighborhoods.name: New Town/Princes Street
neighborhoods.description: Elegant Georgian area with excellent shopping and dining. Very safe with frequent police presence and well-maintained streets. Popular nightlife destination.

neighborhoods.name: Stockbridge
neighborhoods.description: Affluent neighborhood with consistently low crime rates, daily patrols, and peaceful residential atmosphere. Considered one of Edinburgh's safest areas.

neighborhoods.name: Morningside
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area south of city center with excellent safety record, well-lit streets, and strong community presence. Very popular with families.

neighborhoods.name: Leith
neighborhoods.description: Port area undergoing regeneration with higher crime rates including property offenses and drug-related activity. Increased police presence improving conditions.

neighborhoods.name: Sighthill/Craigmillar
neighborhoods.description: Areas with elevated crime rates and reports of antisocial behavior. Visitors should exercise caution and avoid walking alone, especially after dark.

scams.name: Fake Police Officers
scams.description: Criminals impersonating plain-clothes police officers approaching tourists to check belongings or documents.
scams.howToAvoid: Real police will show proper ID. Ask to see warrant cards and verify by calling 101. Legitimate officers rarely conduct random street searches.

scams.name: Fake Tickets
scams.description: Selling counterfeit tickets for attractions, events, or transport online or in person, particularly for Edinburgh Festival events.
scams.howToAvoid: Only buy tickets from official venues, authorized retailers, or verified websites. Be wary of unusually cheap prices or street sellers.

scams.name: Distraction Theft
scams.description: Someone asks for help with directions or photos while accomplices steal belongings, or the mustard/bird droppings distraction technique.
scams.howToAvoid: Stay alert around tourist areas, keep valuables secure, and be cautious of unsolicited help or staged accidents requiring assistance.

scams.name: Overpriced Bar Scam
scams.description: Friendly locals invite tourists to specific bars where drinks are extremely overpriced and payment is enforced by security.
scams.howToAvoid: Suggest your own venue choice, always check drink prices before ordering, and be cautious of overly friendly strangers insisting on specific bars.

soloFemale.overview: Edinburgh is ranked as the 5th safest UK city for women and ranks #22 globally for solo female travelers. Women report feeling secure walking alone even at night, particularly in central areas. The violence rate reported by women is only 12.3%, significantly lower than other UK cities. The friendly Scottish culture and excellent safety infrastructure make it ideal for solo female exploration.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas like Old Town or New Town
Use the buddy system when enjoying Edinburgh's nightlife and keep drinks covered
Trust your instincts and remove yourself from uncomfortable situations
Share your location with family/friends when exploring, especially if hiking Arthur's Seat alone
Avoid walking through parks or peripheral areas very late at night

nightSafety.overview: Edinburgh is generally very safe at night with a safety rating of 62.33 for walking alone after dark. The city center, Royal Mile, Princes Street, and Grassmarket have excellent lighting and regular patrols. Most bars and clubs remain open until the early hours with liberal licensing policies. The main risks are encountering drunk students or tourists rather than serious crime.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas like the city center and main tourist streets
Use official taxis or ride-share apps rather than walking long distances alone
Be cautious around pub closing times when crowds can become boisterous
Avoid poorly lit closes (narrow alleys) and residential areas after midnight
Stay aware of your surroundings and don't accept drinks from strangers

transport.metro: Edinburgh has no metro system but features an extensive tram network connecting the airport to city center with 15 stops. Trams run frequently from 5:30 AM to midnight and are considered very safe and modern.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates effectively in Edinburgh and has partnered with local taxi company Capital Cars for faster service. Generally cheaper than traditional taxis and very reliable with typical wait times under 10 minutes in the city center.
transport.taxis: Over 1,000 licensed black cabs operate with excellent safety standards. Drivers undergo rigorous knowledge tests and background checks. Readily available from ranks or can be hailed on streets when light is on. City Cabs (0131 228 1211) provides reliable advance booking.
transport.tips: Public transport is excellent and operates 24/7. Lothian Buses provide comprehensive coverage with contactless payment and daily fare caps. Walking is often the best option for city center attractions due to Edinburgh's compact, pedestrian-friendly layout.

customs: Queuing (standing in line) is taken very seriously - never push in or skip ahead
Use 'please' and 'thank you' frequently, and begin requests with 'excuse me' or 'sorry to bother you'
Never call a Scottish person English - Scotland has distinct identity and culture
Buy rounds of drinks when in pubs - it's expected that everyone takes turns paying
Avoid talking loudly in public spaces and keep mobile phone conversations brief on transport

health.overview: Edinburgh has excellent healthcare with both NHS and private options available. Medical care is widely available though tourists pay 150% of standard NHS rates. Private clinics exist but often lack emergency departments.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Edinburgh though it's quite hard (high mineral content). Bottled water is widely available if preferred.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Standard travel insurance recommended as medical costs can be significant for non-residents. Check if your insurance covers international medical treatment.
health.altitude: Edinburgh sits at sea level with a temperate maritime climate. Weather can be unpredictable year-round, so pack layers and waterproof clothing regardless of season.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy London covers Scotland: +44 20 7499 9000. For emergencies involving US citizens: +1-202-501-4444 (24/7). Located at 33 Nine Elms Lane, London SW11 7US.

faq.a: Yes, Edinburgh is very safe for tourists with one of the lowest crime rates in the UK at 67.9 offenses per 1,000 people. Violent crime against tourists is rare, and the city has excellent safety infrastructure with regular police patrols in tourist areas.

Edinburgh is generally safe at night, especially in the city center and well-populated areas. The Royal Mile, Princes Street, and Grassmarket have good lighting and regular patrols. However, use standard urban precautions and avoid walking alone through parks or peripheral areas after dark.

faq.q: Is Edinburgh safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Edinburgh is considered one of the best UK cities for solo female travel, ranked #22 globally. Women report feeling comfortable moving around day and night, with only 12.3% reporting any violence concerns - significantly lower than other UK cities.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Edinburgh?
faq.a: Most of Edinburgh is safe, but exercise extra caution in Leith (especially at night), Sighthill, and Craigmillar which have higher crime rates. Avoid poorly lit closes (narrow alleys) late at night and the red-light district between Salamander Street and Leith Link.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Edinburgh?
faq.a: Yes, Edinburgh's public transport is very safe and reliable. Buses operate 24/7, trams are modern and secure, and taxis are well-regulated. The transport system is considered one of the safest aspects of visiting Edinburgh with excellent safety records.
